articles.title: A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
articles.description: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. articles.title: Evaporative Cooling in Data Centers: Why the Industry Hesitates to Move On
articles.description: Evaporative cooling still dominates data centers, but with rising liquid cooling adoption, is the industry ready to embrace more sustainable solutions?

articles.title: The inevitable weakness of metrics
articles.description: There are plenty of useful things a metric can reveal. There are even more it can obscure or corrupt. articles.title: Brain-computer interface trials are taking off
articles.description: This week, I covered the story of Casey Harrell—a man with ALS who is “the first power user” of a brain implant, according to the researchers who worked with him.
